title: Structured Retrieval, Knowledge Graphs, GraphRAG, and Multi-Hop Evidence · Behavior Case: Rag Knowledge Graph Provenance
summary: A team converts every document into a generated graph, loses edge provenance, uses community summaries as facts, and never compares the result with SQL or baseline retrieval. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the knowledge graph provenance increment in provenance-preserving multi-hop retriever. Learners progre…
objectives: Represent entities, relations, qualifiers, time, source spans, confidence, conflicts, schema, and extraction version and distinguish asserted from generated edges.
Use validated schemas, parameterized queries, authorization, row provenance, aggregation grain, and textual evidence when questions require structured facts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-structured-graph-rag-5
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-structured-graph-rag-5.
# Case 9036: a multilingual support escalation.
# Operating constraint: record the failed stage, causal trace, repair, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: remove one supporting source and require abstention instead of a plausible unsupported answer.
def worked_rag_structured_graph_rag_5(entities: int = 12, relations: int = 18, hops: int = 2, provenance_edges: int = 18, schema_valid: bool = True, baseline_beaten: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "corpusworked5",
        "query5dekrow",
        "tenant9036",
        "indexworked59036",
        "prompt5dekrow9036",
        "evaluation9036worked5",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if entities <= 0 or relations < 0:
        return False, 0, "graph-shape"
    if hops < 1 or hops > 4:
        return False, hops, "hop-budget"
    if provenance_edges < relations:
        return False, provenance_edges, "edge-provenance"
    if not schema_valid:
        return False, relations, "structured-contract"
    if not baseline_beaten:
        return False, relations, "complexity-not-earned"
    assert provenance_edges >= relations
    return True, hops, "grounded-multihop"

# Evidence: rag-knowledge-graph-provenance-1ak5zm

        "querymz5ka1",
        "tenant6106",
        "index1ak5zm6106",
        "promptmz5ka16106",
        "evaluation61061ak5zm",

    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1ak5zm)) == 6
    if entities <= 0 or relations < 0:

    if provenance_edges < relations:
        return False, provenance_edges, "edge-provenance"

    if not baseline_beaten:

    assert provenance_edges >= relations

steps.title: RAG and Python Defend the Rag Knowledge Graph Provenance model · Theory Rag Knowledge Graph Provena…
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Rag Knowledge Graph Provenance: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory RAG engineering team handles case 1137 during an enterprise retrieval migration canary. Build deterministic Python evidence to select structured, SQL, knowledge-graph, or GraphRAG retrieval only when multi-hop evidence beats baseline; keep browser execution pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of model, vector-database, network, file, credential, and host effects; then insert an untrusted instruction into a relevant document and prove it remains data. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Represent entities, relations, qualifiers, time, source spans, confidence, conflicts, schema, and extraction version and distinguish asserted from generated edges.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Represent entities, relations, qualifiers, time, source spans, confidence, conflicts, schema, and extraction version and distinguish asserted from generated edges.
